Celine Dionfuelled speculation she will make her musical comeback at the 2024Paris Olympicswhen she was pictured greeting fans in the French capital.

The 56-year-old has not performed live since disclosing her rare stiff person syndrome (SPS), which causes progressive muscular inflexibility.

On Tuesday,Variety first reported thatthe Grammy award winning star arrived in Paris ahead of the Games along with US singer Lady Gaga.

Just hours later, she was then pictured arriving to her hotel and seen greeting fans outside, fuelling the rumours.

The Canadian singer previously teased that a comeback may be imminent in an April interview.

She toldVogue Franceat the time: “I’ve chosen to work with all my body and soul, from head to toe, with a medical team. I want to be the best I can be. My goal is to see the Eiffel Tower again!”

The hitmaker, who revealed her SPS diagnosis in 2022 as she cancelled her Courage World Tour, said she was working everyday to get back on stage.

She continued: “For four years I’ve been saying to myself that I’m not going back, that I’m ready, that I’m not ready… As things stand, I can’t stand here and say to you: ‘Yes, in four months’. I don’t know… My body will tell me.”

It wouldn’t be Dion’s first Olympics peformance, having previously opened the Games in Atlanta, Georgia in 1996 with her track The Power of the Dream.

The Standard has contacted Celine Dion’s rep for comment.

In light of the reports, French PresidentEmmanuel Macronsays he would be “immensely happy” if Dion attended the Paris Olympics 2024 opening ceremony, which will take place in front of the famous Parisian landmark.

When asked about the news, Macron told France 2 in French: “Apparently, she (Dion) has arrived in Paris. It’s great.”

Get ahead with expert services designed to boost business development

When asked about her participating in the ceremony, Macron said it would be “fantastic news” if she did, because Dion is “a great artist”.

He added: “I would be immensely happy if she could be at this opening ceremony, like all our compatriots.”

When pressed by the journalist, he said: “I will not reveal anything, what (Paris Olympics artistic director) Thomas Jolly and all his teams have prepared. There is also a surprise.”

He also said: “I am not responsible for his schedule.”

title: Celine Dion spotted in Paris amid speculation of comeback performance at 2024 Olympic ceremony | The Standard
author: Lisa McLoughlin
description: The singer has not performed live since disclosing her rare stiff person syndrome in 2022
keywords: Celine Dion, Emmanuel Macron, Paris Olympics
